# harbour_certificate (Resource)

Issues and manages a certificate in a Harbour deployment. Destroying this resource revokes the certificate.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "harbour_certificate" "example" {
  common_name = "api.example.internal"
  ttl         = "90d"
  alt_names   = ["api-v2.example.internal"]
}

output "secret_arn" {
  description = "Secrets Manager ARN for the certificate material"
  value       = harbour_certificate.example.secret_arn
}

output "expiry_timestamp" {
  description = "Unix timestamp of certificate expiry"
  value       = harbour_certificate.example.expiry_timestamp
}

# import_to_acm requires ACM import to be configured for this tenant
# (a cross-account IAM role granting harbour-core sts:AssumeRole + acm:ImportCertificate).
# The resulting ARN can be wired directly into AWS resources that expect an ACM cert.
resource "harbour_certificate" "api" {
  common_name   = "api.example.internal"
  ttl           = "90d"
  import_to_acm = true
}

resource "aws_lb_listener" "https" {
  certificate_arn = harbour_certificate.api.acm_certificate_arn
  # ...
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `common_name` (String) Certificate common name (CN).

### Optional

- `alt_names` (List of String) Subject alternative names (SANs).
- `import_to_acm` (Boolean) Import the issued certificate into ACM in the customer account via the tenant's configured cross-account role. Requires ACM import to be configured for this tenant (see harbour-acm-import IAM role).
- `ttl` (String) Certificate TTL (e.g. 90d, 8760h). Defaults to the tenant default_cert_ttl when omitted.

### Read-Only

- `acm_certificate_arn` (String) ARN of the certificate imported into ACM in the customer account. Only set when import_to_acm is true. Usable directly as certificate_arn on AWS resources such as aws_lb_listener.
- `expiry_timestamp` (Number) Certificate expiry as a Unix timestamp.
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `request_id` (String)
- `secret_arn` (String) Secrets Manager ARN where the certificate material is stored.
- `serial_number` (String)
- `status` (String) Current certificate status (requested, issuing, issued, revoked, expired, failed).
